items.myth: Cells run automatically top-to-bottom whenever you open a notebook.
items.reality: You execute cells manually in whatever order you choose; nothing runs until you run it (unless you use Run All).
items.example: Open a notebook and you'll see *cached outputs* from a previous session — the kernel is fresh and no cell has executed yet. The cached outputs are just markup until you re-run.

items.myth: Editing a cell updates results that depended on its old value.
items.reality: Old outputs and kernel state don't change until you re-run the affected cells; stale state lingers.
items.example: Change `k = 5` to `k = 10` in cell 3, but don't re-run. Cell 4's plot (which uses `k`) still shows results for `k = 5` — both in the output below and in the kernel's memory.

items.myth: Restarting the kernel deletes your code.
items.reality: It only clears in-memory state (variables/imports); your cells and saved file remain — you just need to re-run them.
items.example: After Kernel → Restart, all your code cells are still on the page. `print(client)` fails (`NameError`) because the kernel forgot the variable — but the source `client = OpenAI()` in cell 2 is still there to re-execute.
